page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and includes 2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

In benchmark testing, the E48ds_v7 demonstrates strong performance profiles across memory and compression workloads. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and strong marks in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem performance is highlighted by strong read-only small-block cached bandwidth, though large-block uncached read and write operations remain in the average tier. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) contributes to strong scores in PassMark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests, as well as strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium large language models.

While the instance excels at memory-intensive tasks and initial prompt processing, text generation for larger 70B models is a notable bottleneck, registering in the lower performance tier due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Qualitatively, the E48ds_v7 offers high resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring large memory footprints and fast local storage without the overhead of GPU accelerators. This profile is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor and 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, complemented by 2835 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance excels in memory-bound and compression tasks, demonstrating top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong small-block memory bandwidth. Hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX drive strong performance in cryptographic and prompt processing benchmarks. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its efficiency in large-scale LLM text generation. Qualitatively, the E48ds_v7 provides a cost-effective, high-density memory architecture for enterprise workloads, making it highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and large-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering strong performance for databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, built for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-density RAM, fast local storage, and advanced Intel processor extensions for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 resource ratio
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Strong performance in memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
